title: Gentle cell extraction from rocks for downstream single cell sorting and sequencing
author: Jackie Goordial
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bvrmn546

steps.title: Add EDTA to 15 mL tube...
steps.instruction: Add EDTA to 15 mL tube ( containing 5 mL glyTE and 5 g crushed rock), add EDTA for final concentration of 10 mM.

steps.title: Vortex two tubes at time horizontally...
steps.instruction: Vortex two tubes at time horizontally in tube holder of the Vortex-Genie-2. Vortex for 5 min at speed 7 (too high of a speed could allow the rock particles to cause damage to the cells)

steps.title: Sonicate, two tubes at a time...
steps.instruction: Sonicate, two tubes at a time in ice water, for 1 Minute

steps.title: Centrifuge at 1,000 RPM for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 1,000 RPM for 5 minutes to pellet rock particles. Transfer the supernatant to a fresh 15 ml Falcon tube

steps.title: Centrifuge the supernatant 2ml at time...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the supernatant 2ml at time @ 10,000 RPM for 5 minutes in 2 x 2 ml centrifuge tubes. One tube for sorting, and another as replicate or microscopy. Approximately 2.5 ml will be pelleted in each tube over two rounds of centrifugation (2 ml, 2ml, ~ 250 ul). Remove supernatant each time, with caution, using a pipette. Do Not Pour Off Supernatant, cellular material is not always well pelleted and can be lost easily.

steps.title: a
steps.instruction: a. For one tube (for later sorting) Resuspend the pellet in 1ml of 1X GlyTE. Can dilute with 1X PBS. Store in freezer. b. For second tube (for microscopy) – stain with SYBR Green, and filter on 0.2 um polycarbonate filter, rinse and dry filter, then mount on glass slide with Citifluor antifade mounting solution. Count cells, or store slide at 4C until ready to count cells
